metadata.title:
MRS, PRAGER


ENDORSED BY


UNION LABOR

pages.raw_text:
Endorsement of Spokane's great
body of organized labor was Sc.
cured last night by Mrs. III. M.
Prayer, one of the candidates for
job on Spokane's school board.
Mrs. Prayer is already enjoying
the endorsement of the Womens
club, and now she Is to be assist
ed in her campaign as school d.
rector by a committee Of 10 from
the Central Labor union.


The endorsement of the Central
Labor union was rendered Mrs.
Prayer by a popular vote of dele
gates in that organization at the
regular meeting of the labor coun
Gil last night. 1t Is explained by
members of the central body that
the campaign for Mrs. Prayer by
the committee of 10 is to be
voluntary.


Mrs. Prayer comes to Spokane
highly recommended, II Is claimed
she having served on the board OF
school directors in Portland, Ore,
prior to her residence in Spokane.
